lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: Oh! You must be the detective. I wasn't expecting anyone so soon.

lines.speaker: Nancy
lines.text: Nancy Drew. I came as quickly as I could. Can you tell me what happened?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: It's the mistress — Lady Thornwood. She's been missing since Tuesday. The police say she left on her own, but I know better.

choices.text: What makes you think something happened to her?
Who else lives in the manor?
I'd like to look around first.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: She would never leave without telling me. And there were... arguments. Loud ones, in the study, the night before she vanished.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: Her nephew, Mr. Graves. He's been staying here for weeks. Something about the inheritance.

choices.text: Where can I find Mr. Graves?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: Just myself, the gardener Mr. Chen, and Lady Thornwood's nephew — Mr. Graves. He arrived a month ago, uninvited.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: There's also Miss Whitfield, Lady Thornwood's solicitor. She visits weekly.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: He keeps to the garden pavilion mostly. But he was in the study this morning — I heard drawers opening.

lines.speaker: Nancy
lines.text: Thank you, Mrs. Pembrook. That's very helpful.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Pembrook
lines.text: Be careful, dear. This house has more secrets than rooms.
